hero.title: The Operating System for
hero.description: ObjectStack is the open-source AI-native business framework: define objects, permissions, workflows, APIs, UI metadata, and agent tools once as structured Zod metadata. Run governed business operations with ObjectOS, render developer-controlled interfaces with ObjectUI, and give AI agents permission-aware tools instead of raw database access.

builtForAi.title: Built for AI, not retrofitted.
builtForAi.lead: A typical enterprise application is tens of thousands of lines of CRUD, forms, queries, permissions, and API glue spread across a dozen files. ObjectStack collapses the same surface into a few hundred lines of typed metadata — roughly two orders of magnitude less code for a developer to write and maintain.
builtForAi.pullquote: The point isn't lines of code. The point is fit in an agent's context window.
builtForAi.closer: When the entire business system is small, typed, and declarative, an AI agent can load it end-to-end, reason about every dependency, and safely refactor across data, API, UI, and permissions in a single change. That turns AI from an autocomplete tool into a real co-maintainer of production business software.
builtForAi.statAfterMeta: typed metadata × one schema × one context window

ecosystem.title: ObjectStack Core. Business Runtime. Frontend UI.
ecosystem.subtitle: ObjectStack is moving toward one clearer center: an open-source AI-native framework where business metadata, APIs, UI surfaces, and agent tools share one source of truth. ObjectQL remains an internal data/query engine, not the product headline.

protocol.label: Open-Source AI Framework
protocol.name: ObjectStack
protocol.description: Start here when you want the open-source project: Zod metadata for objects, permissions, flows, views, actions, agents, APIs, SDKs, MCP tools, and drivers. ObjectQL is the underlying data/query engine inside ObjectStack, kept deliberately behind the broader framework story.

runtime.label: Business Runtime
runtime.name: ObjectOS
runtime.description: ObjectOS is for business users and operators who need internal tools, approval flows, audit trails, and AI-assisted work running in their own environment. AI agents operate through governed actions with the same identity, permission, and audit model as people.

view.label: Frontend UI Engine
view.name: ObjectUI
view.description: ObjectUI is for frontend developers who want schema-driven React interfaces without giving up control. It renders the same metadata that powers APIs and AI tools, so human screens and agent actions stay aligned.

features.title: Built for AI-Native Business Operations
features.subtitle: ObjectStack makes the business system explicit enough for humans, services, and AI agents to operate safely under one permission model.

localFirst.title: Runs Inside Your Boundary
localFirst.description: ObjectOS can run on your laptop, private cloud, on-prem cluster, or air-gapped network. Business records, identities, sessions, audit logs, files, and prompts stay on infrastructure you control.

databaseAgnostic.title: Drivers Without Migration Theater
databaseAgnostic.description: Postgres, MySQL, SQLite, MongoDB, spreadsheets, SaaS APIs, and custom systems are mounted through drivers. The data/query engine stays behind the scenes; the business contract remains ObjectStack metadata.

protocolDriven.title: Metadata Agents Can Actually Read
protocolDriven.description: Objects, flows, permissions, views, dashboards, actions, agents, and packages compile into versioned artifacts. They are diffable, reviewable, reloadable, and small enough for AI agents to reason about end to end.

typeSafe.title: One Source for UI, APIs, and AI Tools
typeSafe.description: ObjectStack starts from Zod schemas and derives TypeScript types, JSON Schema, REST contracts, UI metadata, SDKs, and MCP tool signatures from the same source of truth.

scale.title: One Runtime, Many Capabilities
scale.description: The microkernel loads plugins for auth, security, audit, storage, jobs, queues, webhooks, AI, and drivers only when needed. A small ObjectOS process can serve standalone apps or many project kernels.

aiNative.title: AI Tools With Runtime Guardrails
aiNative.description: Every approved object/action can become an MCP tool with the same permissions as the signed-in user. Agents do real work through ObjectOS with request IDs, audit logs, policy checks, and no raw SQL handoff.

protocolLongevity.title: Metadata Lasts Decades. Frameworks Don't.
protocolLongevity.subtitle: Business definitions should outlive your framework choices. Invest in structured Zod metadata, not in this year's hot runtime.
protocolLongevity.insight: 💡 ObjectStack treats your business as Zod metadata — versioned, signed, replayable. When today's runtime is replaced, your objects, flows, policies, and MCP tool definitions move with you intact.

howItWorks.title: Metadata-Driven Architecture in Action
howItWorks.subtitle: From ObjectStack metadata to compiled artifacts to a self-hosted runtime with APIs, Console UI, and AI tools.
howItWorks.demoDescription: See how ObjectStack metadata becomes a governed AI-operable application

step1.title: Define: Business Metadata in ObjectStack
step1.description: Objects, fields, relations, validation, permissions, views, flows, agents, and packages are written as structured metadata. The data/query engine is internal plumbing; the public contract is ObjectStack.

step2.title: Execute: The Microkernel Runtime
step2.description: ObjectOS boots a per-project kernel from the compiled artifact, loads only the enabled capabilities, and enforces auth, RBAC, row-level rules, field redaction, validation, AI tool boundaries, and audit uniformly.

step3.title: Render: Apps, APIs, and Agent Tools
step3.description: The same artifact powers generated REST endpoints, SDK calls, ObjectUI views, Console surfaces, and MCP tools. Human users and AI agents operate the same governed backend.

useCases.title: From Prototype to Mission-Critical Systems
useCases.subtitle: Use the same metadata model for local internal tools, self-hosted enterprise apps, and AI-operated workflows.

individualDevelopers.title: Internal Tools & Local-First Apps
individualDevelopers.description: Start a local ObjectOS runtime with SQLite, generated REST, Console, Account, audit, and metadata-driven UI. Prototype internal tools without waiting on cloud infrastructure.

enterpriseIT.title: Heterogeneous Data Unification & Shadow IT
enterpriseIT.description: Wrap existing databases, spreadsheets, and SaaS APIs as governed objects. Apply RBAC, row-level rules, field redaction, audit, and MCP exposure without forcing a big-bang migration.

saasStartups.title: Agent-Ready SaaS Foundation
saasStartups.description: Ship vertical SaaS with tenants, permissions, generated APIs, a React console, and AI tools from the first version. Run managed, self-hosted, or air-gapped using the same artifact model.
saasStartups.stats: Multi-tenant + agent-ready out of the box

lowCodeVendors.title: Platform Engineering & Open-Core Products
lowCodeVendors.description: Use ObjectStack as the open framework underneath your own platform: Zod metadata, plugin runtime, drivers, ObjectUI renderers, and agent tools. Keep your product experience differentiated.

faq.title: Questions from Decision Makers
faq.subtitle: Strategic and technical considerations for CTOs and engineering leaders.

items.question: How does this reduce our total cost of ownership?
items.answer: It removes repeated glue: one metadata definition drives objects, APIs, SDKs, UI, permissions, and MCP tools. ObjectOS supplies the runtime plumbing (auth, security, audit, jobs, webhooks, AI), while ObjectUI supplies reusable renderers instead of per-app CRUD screens.

items.question: What's our migration path from existing systems?
items.answer: Incremental. Mount one existing table, spreadsheet, or SaaS API through a driver, define its ObjectStack metadata, and expose only the governed surfaces you need. ObjectQL remains a useful data engine, but you do not have to rewrite the whole system around it.

items.question: How do we handle compliance and audit?
items.answer: Every action — UI, API, SDK, or MCP tool — is logged with principal (user or agent), intent, and resulting metadata change. Field-level permissions are declared in Zod, not bolted on. Deploy on-premise for jurisdictions requiring strict residency.

items.question: Can we deploy in air-gapped or edge environments?
items.answer: Yes. The kernel + artifact bundle runs offline. Deploy on field devices, manufacturing floors, or secure facilities. Sync when connectivity returns. The MCP surface continues to work for local AI tooling.

items.question: What's the performance impact compared to native queries?
items.answer: ObjectOS routes data access through drivers that issue native SQL or native database/API calls. Hot paths can still use custom actions or driver-specific logic while retaining the metadata contract for permissions, UI, and agent tools.

items.question: How does this fit with our existing stack?
items.answer: The framework is TypeScript-first and Zod-based, ObjectOS is a Node.js runtime, and ObjectUI is React-based. Other services can interact through generated REST APIs and MCP tools, so the runtime can sit beside existing systems rather than replace them all at once.

items.question: What's the roadmap for enterprise features like SAML SSO?
items.answer: Auth, OAuth/OIDC, RBAC, RLS, FLS, and audit log are in the open-core today (Apache-2.0). On the roadmap toward ObjectCloud GA (July 2026) and beyond: SAML/SCIM, SIEM export, advanced flow orchestration, SOC 2 attestation. Enterprise customers shape prioritization — join the waitlist to discuss requirements.

items.question: How do we make this maintainable long-term?
items.answer: Zod metadata is plain TypeScript — no proprietary DSL. Types are inferred end-to-end. Artifact bundles are diffable and replayable. Your team owns the metadata; runtimes are replaceable.

cta.headline2: Let Humans And Agents Operate It Safely.
cta.description: Stop scattering business logic across SQL, JavaScript, and UI state. Make objects, permissions, workflows, APIs, UI metadata, and agent tools one structured Zod source of truth — analyzable, versioned, and auditable end to end. Launch July 2026.

footer.tagline: The operating system for AI-native business.

notFound.subtitle: Page Not Found
notFound.description: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

hero.headline: Any Data Source. One API.
hero.subheadline: The Universal Adapter for Modern Business Data
hero.description: ObjectStack acts as a universal adapter. Write your business logic once in ObjectQL, and run it on Postgres, Redis, MongoDB, or Excel. If you have a legacy proprietary system, write a Driver, not a migration script.

driverRegistry.title: Driver Registry
driverRegistry.subtitle: This is NOT just a list of logos. It is a Driver Registry connecting any data source to one unified API.

postgres.name: PostgreSQL
postgres.description: Production-grade relational database with JSONB and full SQL compliance.

mysql.name: MySQL
mysql.description: High-concurrency relational database optimized for web-scale workloads.

redis.name: Redis
redis.description: In-memory data structure store used as database, cache, and message broker.

excel.name: Excel / CSV
excel.description: Mount spreadsheets alongside your databases and query via unified API.

csv.name: CSV Files
csv.description: Read and write CSV files with full ObjectQL query support.

custom.title: Custom: Build Your Own Driver in TypeScript
custom.description: Extend ObjectStack with custom drivers for proprietary systems, legacy databases, or SaaS APIs. Write once in TypeScript, deploy anywhere.

categories.title: Explore the Driver Marketplace
categories.subtitle: Discover community-built drivers, themes, and plugins for ObjectStack.

customDriver.title: Build Your Own Driver
customDriver.subtitle: Extend ObjectStack with custom drivers for any data source—legacy systems, proprietary databases, or third-party APIs.

step1.title: Implement the Driver Interface
step1.description: Write TypeScript code implementing the ObjectQL Driver Protocol. Define how to read, write, and query your data source.

step2.title: Test Locally with Studio
step2.description: Load your custom driver in Object Studio. Test queries, validate performance, and iterate rapidly.

step3.title: Publish to Registry
step3.description: Share your driver with the community or keep it private for your organization. Versioning and dependency management included.

hero.label: Query Engine
hero.title: ObjectQL
hero.subtitle: The query and driver engine inside ObjectStack
hero.description: ObjectQL is the internal query engine and driver contract used by ObjectStack. It normalizes filtering, sorting, pagination, aggregation, relationship expansion, and data-source drivers so business objects can run across SQL databases, document stores, spreadsheets, SaaS APIs, and proprietary systems without changing the application metadata.

features.title: What ObjectQL Does
features.subtitle: A portable query contract for heterogeneous enterprise data sources.

queryModel.title: Unified Query Shape
queryModel.description: Filters, sorting, pagination, joins, expansion, aggregation, and projections are represented as structured query metadata instead of route-specific SQL strings.

driverContract.title: Driver Contract
driverContract.description: Drivers translate ObjectQL operations into native SQL, MongoDB pipelines, spreadsheet reads, SaaS API calls, or custom enterprise protocols while keeping the object contract stable.

permissionAware.title: Permission-Aware Execution
permissionAware.description: ObjectOS can apply tenant isolation, row-level rules, field redaction, audit, and agent identity around every ObjectQL-backed operation.

aiReadable.title: Readable for AI Tools
aiReadable.description: Queries are structured enough for generated SDKs, REST APIs, ObjectUI views, and MCP tools to expose safe data access without handing agents raw SQL.

useCases.title: Where ObjectQL Fits
useCases.subtitle: ObjectQL is infrastructure, not the whole framework. Use it where data access must remain portable and governed.

federatedData.title: Federated Enterprise Data
federatedData.description: Mount Postgres, MongoDB, Excel, and legacy systems behind one query contract so applications can read them through consistent object metadata.

driverDevelopment.title: Custom Driver Development
driverDevelopment.description: Implement a driver for a proprietary database, SaaS API, mainframe, or file format and let ObjectStack expose it as governed objects.

aiDataAccess.title: AI Data Access Boundary
aiDataAccess.description: Expose permission-aware list, search, aggregate, and lookup tools to agents while preserving request attribution and audit trails.

example.title: From portable query to native execution
example.subtitle: ObjectQL describes intent; each driver executes it in the language of its data source.

comparison.title: ObjectQL vs. Raw SQL / ORM Queries
comparison.subtitle: ObjectQL is a runtime query contract for ObjectStack, not a replacement for every low-level database tool.

runtimeOverhead.objectql: Portable query metadata across drivers and object surfaces.
runtimeOverhead.orm: Usually tied to one database dialect, model layer, or application service.

security.objectql: Designed to run behind ObjectOS permissions, audit, redaction, and tenant boundaries.
security.orm: Governance is often enforced manually in each route or service method.

portability.objectql: Driver can translate the same query shape to SQL, documents, spreadsheets, or APIs.
portability.orm: Switching storage often changes models, migrations, and query code.

virtualColumns.objectql: Structured query inputs can become safe MCP tools.
virtualColumns.orm: Agents usually need custom tool wrappers or risky SQL access.

drivers.title: Driver Targets
drivers.subtitle: ObjectQL becomes useful when drivers make different data sources feel governable through one object contract.

postgresql.name: PostgreSQL
postgresql.description: Relational production storage and analytics-friendly queries.

mysql.name: MySQL
mysql.description: Common relational storage for web and enterprise applications.

sqlite.name: SQLite
sqlite.description: Local development, embedded tools, and standalone deployments.

sqlserver.name: SQL Server
sqlserver.description: Enterprise relational estates through commercial or custom drivers.

oracle.name: Oracle Database
oracle.description: Legacy systems can be wrapped without immediate migration.

mongodb.name: MongoDB
mongodb.description: Document stores through native aggregation semantics where appropriate.

excel.name: Excel / CSV
excel.description: Spreadsheets become queryable governed data sources.

hero.label: Business Runtime
hero.title: ObjectOS
hero.subtitle: A self-hosted business platform your team can operate
hero.description: ObjectOS is for business users, operators, and IT teams who need internal tools, approval workflows, governed AI actions, audit logs, and data ownership without sending the business system to a third-party cloud. AI assistants act through the same controlled business actions as people.

commandCenter.title: The Business Platform AI Can Actually Use
commandCenter.subtitle: ObjectOS turns metadata-defined apps into tools business teams can run, govern, and audit.
commandCenter.description: Put CRM records, contracts, tickets, approvals, and internal tools on ObjectOS. Your people and AI assistants act through the same identity, permission, and audit model, so business operations stay governed even when agents help execute the work.

pillars.title: What Business Teams Get from ObjectOS
pillars.subtitle: Self-hosted apps, generated admin surfaces, Account, permissions, audit, approvals, and AI assistance without losing data control.

identityGovernance.title: Business Access Control
identityGovernance.description: Users sign in through the identity system you already trust. Roles, row rules, and field redaction protect records whether work happens in the Console, an API, or an AI-assisted action.

workflowOrchestration.title: Approvals and Internal Tools
workflowOrchestration.description: Tickets, contracts, expenses, onboarding, inventory, and custom workflows become governed business apps with generated APIs and Console surfaces.

localFirstSync.title: Data Stays in Your Environment
localFirstSync.description: Business records, identities, sessions, audit logs, files, and prompts stay in your database and storage. Offline and air-gapped deployments remain first-class options.

pluginArchitecture.title: Extend Without Replatforming
pluginArchitecture.description: Add drivers, identity providers, audit exporters, webhooks, storage, jobs, and AI capabilities as plugins while keeping the same business runtime.

manifest.title: Architecture as Metadata
manifest.subtitle: Flows and plugins are declarative artifacts loaded by the kernel — analyzable by humans and AI agents alike.
manifest.description: Stop writing imperative code for patterns the kernel already understands. Express intent as metadata; ObjectOS executes it, audits it, and exposes it to agents as MCP tools.
manifest.workflowCode:
# expense-approval.flow.yml
name: ExpenseApproval
initialState: submitted

states:
  submitted:
    on:
      approve:
        target: approved
        conditions:
          - role: manager
          - amount: < 5000
      escalate:
        target: pending_director
        conditions:
          - amount: >= 5000
  
  pending_director:
    on:
      approve:
        target: approved
        conditions:
          - role: director
      reject:
        target: rejected
  
  approved:
    actions:
      - notify: submitter
      - update_ledger
      - archive
  
  rejected:
    actions:
      - notify: submitter
      - log_audit

# Same flow becomes:
#   • a REST endpoint
#   • a typed SDK call
#   • an MCP tool 'expense.approve' for AI agents

localFirstDeepDive.title: Local-First Sync, Built In
localFirstDeepDive.subtitle: Offline-capable business apps are a kernel property, not a third-party library.
localFirstDeepDive.problemDescription: Field technicians, sales reps, and operators in disconnected environments work against local data that diverges from the server. Merging by hand is error-prone — who wins, what gets lost, how do you audit it?
localFirstDeepDive.solutionDescription: The kernel acts as the authoritative replication controller between the server and local stores (SQLite/RxDB). CRDT and last-write-wins strategies are declared as policy metadata; the kernel handles execution and conflict resolution. Every merge is attributable and auditable.
benefits: Zero-latency interactions for offline users and agents
Automatic conflict resolution — no hand-rolled merge code
Bi-directional sync with intact audit trail

ecosystem.title: Three Layers, One Metadata Spine
ecosystem.subtitle: Most customers deploy ObjectOS first; Framework and ObjectUI explain where the artifact and UI surfaces come from.

objectqlRole.title: Framework / ObjectQL
objectqlRole.description: Defines objects, fields, queries, drivers, flows, views, actions, and agents as structured metadata artifacts.

objectuiRole.title: ObjectUI
objectuiRole.description: Renders the Console, app shell, object views, forms, dashboards, and plugin views from metadata.

objectosRole.title: ObjectOS
objectosRole.description: Runs the artifact in your environment, enforces identity and permissions, and exposes APIs plus MCP tools.

ecosystem.integration: ObjectOS is the runtime you operate; Framework metadata and ObjectUI renderers plug into it.

useCases.title: From Local-First Tools to Mission-Critical Platforms
useCases.subtitle: One microkernel that scales from a single-user workstation to multi-tenant agent-operated platforms.

fieldOperations.title: Field & Offline Operations
fieldOperations.description: Local-first apps for technicians, inspectors, and sales teams. The kernel handles offline writes, sync, and conflict resolution. Audit and attribution are preserved end to end, even when an AI assistant performs the action.

enterpriseApps.title: Enterprise Application Suite
enterpriseApps.description: Run ERP / CRM / HRM modules as plugins on a single kernel. Unified identity, unified audit, unified MCP surface so internal copilots can safely act across modules with role-bound permissions.

multiTenantSaaS.title: Agent-Operated Multi-Tenant SaaS
multiTenantSaaS.description: Ship SaaS where every tenant gets the same auditable surface — REST, SDK, and MCP — backed by per-tenant policies in metadata. Scale from 10 to 10,000 tenants without parallel security or AI-tool implementations.

hero.label: Frontend Developer Toolkit
hero.title: ObjectUI
hero.subtitle: Schema-driven React interfaces for serious frontend teams
hero.description: ObjectUI is for frontend developers: render objects, forms, dashboards, pages, and plugin views from ObjectStack metadata while keeping React, Tailwind, Shadcn, custom routing, custom auth, and your own backend under your control. The same metadata also powers APIs and AI tools.

features.title: Why ObjectUI?
features.subtitle: A modular frontend toolkit: use the full Console when useful, or compose app-shell/provider packages inside your own React application.

theStack.title: Built for React Teams
theStack.description: Use familiar React primitives, Tailwind utilities, Radix/Shadcn components, and your existing design system. ObjectUI renders from metadata without trapping you in a black box.

serverDriven.title: Metadata Without Losing Control
serverDriven.description: Views, pages, dashboards, forms, fields, and actions can come from metadata, while routing, auth, layout boundaries, and component overrides stay in your frontend code.

enterpriseReady.title: Console or Embedded Shell
enterpriseReady.description: Run the complete ObjectStack Console, or embed @object-ui/app-shell and @object-ui/providers for a small custom interface connected to your REST, GraphQL, or ObjectOS backend.

extensible.title: Frontend Plugin Surface
extensible.description: Register custom fields, widgets, view types, and plugins such as kanban, calendar, charts, reports, maps, AI, and workflow views.

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.subtitle: From admin panels to AI-operated dashboards.

admin.title: Admin Surfaces & Internal Tools
admin.description: Render complete CRUD and review interfaces from the same Zod-defined objects that back your APIs. Junior engineers and AI agents read from the same metadata — UI drift becomes impossible.

forms.title: Dynamic Forms & Approval Flows
forms.description: Multi-step wizards, conditional logic, and validation are metadata, not bespoke state machines. Forms surface the same business actions exposed to agents as MCP tools — same rules, same audit.

dataViz.title: Data-Heavy Operator Consoles
dataViz.description: Virtualized tables, dashboards, and analytics views that scale to millions of rows. Operators and agentic copilots share a consistent, permissioned view of the business.

magicTrick.title: View as Metadata
magicTrick.subtitle: Declare metadata; ObjectUI renders a real interface through the app shell and providers.
magicTrick.output:
A fully functional, permission-aware data table:

• Shadcn UI components, beautiful by default
• Sortable / filterable columns derived from object metadata
• Row-level permissions: agents and humans see the same scope
• Actions also exposed as MCP tools (user.edit, user.delete)
• Pagination, global search, responsive layout
• Accessible by default (ARIA, keyboard navigation)
• Tailwind classes — fully customizable

architecture.title: How It Works
architecture.subtitle: Metadata -> providers -> app shell/renderers, with your own backend or ObjectOS.

step1.title: Step 1: Metadata
step1.description: Objects, views, dashboards, pages, fields, labels, and actions are structured definitions that can be loaded lazily and localized by convention.

step2.title: Step 2: Providers
step2.description: DataSourceProvider, MetadataProvider, and ThemeProvider bridge ObjectUI to ObjectOS or to your own REST/GraphQL/custom API.

step3.title: Step 3: Renderers
step3.description: AppShell, ObjectRenderer, DashboardRenderer, PageRenderer, and FormRenderer map metadata to accessible React components.

extensibility.title: Production-Grade Extensibility
extensibility.subtitle: ObjectUI is a foundation you build on, not a cage.
extensibility.description: Register custom React components into the engine and reference them from view metadata. Custom widgets inherit validation, permissions, and audit from the same Zod-defined business objects.

example.title: Custom Component Example

ecosystem.title: Part of ObjectStack
ecosystem.subtitle: The View Layer of an AI-native business backend.
ecosystem.description: ObjectUI works independently with any backend via providers, but it aligns naturally with ObjectOS and ObjectStack metadata. ObjectQL is only the data engine behind some of those objects, not a requirement for every UI integration.
ecosystem.integrated: Full ObjectOS Console and ObjectStack metadata integration

hero.label: Cloud Development
hero.title: ObjectCloud
hero.subtitle: Cloud workspace for building enterprise AI apps with ObjectStack
hero.description: ObjectCloud is the upcoming cloud development and runtime environment for ObjectStack. Developers will create projects, manage metadata, preview ObjectOS runtimes, test MCP tools, and promote versioned artifacts to managed or self-hosted environments. Opening soon for early teams.

features.title: What ObjectCloud Will Provide
features.subtitle: A developer workspace first, with production runtime options behind it.

hostedWorkspace.title: Hosted ObjectStack Workspace
hostedWorkspace.description: Create ObjectStack projects in the browser, manage environments, and keep metadata versions, secrets, and deployment artifacts in one governed workspace.

runtimePreview.title: Runtime Preview
runtimePreview.description: Launch a preview ObjectOS runtime for each project version. Inspect generated APIs, permissions, audit logs, ObjectUI screens, and MCP tools before promotion.

artifactPromotion.title: Artifact Promotion
artifactPromotion.description: Promote the same signed metadata bundle from development to staging and production. Environment differences live in config, not in rewritten application code.

openingSoon.title: Opening Soon
openingSoon.description: ObjectCloud is not generally available yet. Early access will focus on developer teams building AI-operated internal tools and enterprise SaaS backends.

useCases.title: Cloud Development Use Cases
useCases.subtitle: For teams that want ObjectStack development without setting up infrastructure first.

teamDevelopment.title: Team Metadata Development
teamDevelopment.description: Collaborate on objects, permissions, views, actions, and agent tools with shared environments and reviewable metadata versions.

aiToolTesting.title: MCP Tool Testing
aiToolTesting.description: Preview generated agent tools against a permission-aware runtime before exposing them to production copilots or agents.

deploymentReadiness.title: Deployment Readiness
deploymentReadiness.description: Package ObjectStack artifacts for ObjectCloud managed runtime, self-hosted ObjectOS, or private enterprise infrastructure.

workflow.title: From Cloud Workspace to Runtime
workflow.subtitle: Develop in the cloud, ship the same artifact anywhere.

step1.title: Create a Project
step1.description: Start from a template or repository and define objects, views, permissions, actions, and agent tools.

step2.title: Preview the Runtime
step2.description: Run a project version in an isolated ObjectOS preview environment with API, UI, audit, and MCP tool surfaces.

step3.title: Promote or Export
step3.description: Deploy to managed ObjectCloud, export to self-hosted ObjectOS, or hand the artifact to enterprise infrastructure teams.

noVendorLockIn.title: Portable by Design
noVendorLockIn.subtitle: Cloud development should not trap the application.
noVendorLockIn.description: ObjectCloud works with ObjectStack artifacts: objects, policies, actions, views, and agent tools. Teams can export the same metadata bundle and run it in self-hosted environments when business or compliance requirements demand it.

ecosystem.title: Part of the Developer Toolchain
ecosystem.subtitle: Cloud, Studio, and CLI are separate entry points.
ecosystem.description: ObjectCloud is the browser-based cloud workspace. Studio is the visual metadata development environment. CLI is the command-line automation tool for local development, CI/CD, and operations.
ecosystem.opensource: Built around the open ObjectStack framework
ecosystem.studio: Import and preview metadata authored in Studio
ecosystem.enterprise: Export artifacts for private runtime deployment

hero.label: Visual Metadata IDE
hero.title: Object Studio
hero.subtitle: Visual development environment for ObjectStack metadata
hero.description: Object Studio is a visual development environment for developers and architects who want to model ObjectStack metadata directly: objects, fields, relationships, permissions, views, actions, and agent tools. It is not the CLI and not the cloud platform; it is the visual workspace for understanding and editing the business system.

features.title: What Studio Is For
features.subtitle: Make metadata visible, reviewable, and easier to reason about.

visualDesigner.title: Visual Metadata Modeling
visualDesigner.description: Inspect and edit object schemas, fields, relationships, permissions, view metadata, and action definitions without losing the underlying typed source of truth.

localFirst.title: Local / Workspace Preview
localFirst.description: Preview how metadata becomes ObjectOS runtime surfaces, generated APIs, ObjectUI screens, and MCP tools before shipping a versioned artifact.

integratedCLI.title: Pairs With CLI, Not Replaces It
integratedCLI.description: Studio helps humans understand and edit metadata visually. CLI remains the independent automation surface for scaffolding, validation, CI/CD, and deployment.

hotReload.title: Metadata Feedback Loop
hotReload.description: Change object, permission, view, or agent-tool metadata and inspect the resulting runtime surface quickly, with fewer round trips through raw files.

useCases.title: Studio Use Cases
useCases.subtitle: For developers who need a visual map of a metadata-defined system.

rapidPrototyping.title: Model New Business Domains
rapidPrototyping.description: Explore objects, relationships, permissions, and UI projections before committing to a full implementation.

enterpriseApps.title: Review Enterprise Metadata
enterpriseApps.description: Help platform teams, architects, and developers inspect the same metadata model during design review and implementation.

edgeDeployment.title: Preview Generated UI and Tools
edgeDeployment.description: See how metadata renders into ObjectUI screens and how actions appear as agent-ready tool surfaces.

step1.title: Open Metadata
step1.description: Load a project or cloud workspace and inspect objects, views, actions, permissions, and agent tools.

step2.title: Edit Visually
step2.description: Modify metadata through visual forms and diagrams while preserving typed source artifacts.

step3.title: Preview Runtime Effects
step3.description: Check generated API, UI, and tool surfaces before handing the project to CLI or Cloud for promotion.

cliPower.title: CLI Is Independent
cliPower.subtitle: Use CLI for automation; use Studio for visual understanding.
cliPower.description: Studio should not be treated as a wrapper around CLI. It is a visual metadata environment. CLI remains the separate command-line interface for project scaffolding, validation, build, deployment, and operations.

examples.description: Create a project from the terminal
Promote an artifact to a runtime

ecosystem.title: Visual Layer of the Developer Toolchain
ecosystem.subtitle: Studio complements ObjectCloud and CLI.
ecosystem.description: Use Studio when the team needs to see and edit metadata visually. Use CLI when the same metadata needs to be automated. Use ObjectCloud when the team wants a hosted development workspace and runtime preview.
ecosystem.selfHosted: Preview artifacts destined for self-hosted ObjectOS

hero.label: Command-Line Tool
hero.title: ObjectStack CLI
hero.subtitle: Independent CLI for ObjectStack development, CI/CD, and operations
hero.description: ObjectStack CLI is a standalone command-line tool for developers and platform teams. It scaffolds projects, validates metadata, generates types and tool contracts, builds versioned artifacts, runs local previews, and deploys to ObjectCloud or self-hosted ObjectOS. It is separate from Studio and designed for scripts, CI/CD, and agents.

features.title: What CLI Does
features.subtitle: Stable primitives for developers, CI pipelines, and coding agents.

infrastructureAsCode.title: Project Scaffolding
infrastructureAsCode.description: Create ObjectStack projects, add objects, actions, views, agents, drivers, and runtime configuration from repeatable templates.

cicdIntegration.title: Validate and Generate
cicdIntegration.description: Validate metadata, generate TypeScript types, JSON Schema, SDK contracts, and MCP tool definitions as part of development or CI.

multiEnvironment.title: Build and Promote Artifacts
multiEnvironment.description: Package metadata into versioned artifacts and promote the same bundle across local, cloud, staging, and production runtimes.

scriptableAutomation.title: Scriptable Operations
scriptableAutomation.description: Use JSON output, predictable exit codes, and composable commands for CI/CD, GitOps, release automation, and agent-driven maintenance.

commands.title: Representative Commands
commands.subtitle: Command names will follow the framework CLI in the open-source repo.

init.details: Scaffold objects, views, actions, agents, drivers, and runtime config.

schema.details: Check object, permission, view, action, and agent-tool metadata before build or deploy.

migrate.details: Generate types, JSON Schema, SDK contracts, and MCP tool definitions.

deploy.details: Package metadata for ObjectCloud or self-hosted ObjectOS.

env.details: Promote a built artifact to local, cloud, staging, production, or self-hosted runtime.

driver.details: Check data source drivers before runtime promotion.

step1.title: Scaffold
step1.description: Create a project and add typed metadata files for objects, views, actions, agents, and drivers.

step2.title: Validate and Build
step2.description: Check metadata, generate derived types and tool contracts, and package a versioned artifact.

step3.title: Run or Deploy
step3.description: Start a local preview, publish to ObjectCloud, or deploy to self-hosted ObjectOS.

useCases.title: CLI Use Cases
useCases.subtitle: Where an independent command line matters.

cicdPipelines.title: CI/CD Pipelines
cicdPipelines.description: Run metadata validation, type generation, test runtime startup, and artifact promotion on every pull request or release.

multiTenantSaaS.title: Project and Environment Automation
multiTenantSaaS.description: Automate environment setup, secrets, driver checks, artifact exports, and deployment tasks without opening a visual tool.

edgeDeployment.title: Local and Self-Hosted Development
edgeDeployment.description: Run ObjectStack locally, package artifacts for self-hosted ObjectOS, and manage deployments that cannot depend on a browser workspace.

disasterRecovery.title: Agent-Friendly Tooling
disasterRecovery.description: Give coding agents predictable commands for scaffold, validate, build, diff, and deploy operations.

installation.title: Installation
installation.subtitle: Distribution details will follow the framework repository.

homebrew.description: Create a project without global install

docker.description: CLI source lives in the open-source framework repo

ecosystem.title: Independent Part of the Toolchain
ecosystem.subtitle: CLI is not Studio. Studio is not CLI.
ecosystem.description: CLI is the scriptable interface for ObjectStack. Studio is visual metadata development. ObjectCloud is cloud workspace and runtime preview. All three work with the same metadata artifacts.
ecosystem.cloud: ObjectCloud for hosted workspace and runtime preview
ecosystem.selfHosted: CLI for local, CI/CD, and self-hosted runtime operations

hero.label: Open-Source Framework
hero.title: ObjectStack
hero.subtitle: The AI-native application framework behind the stack
hero.description: ObjectStack is the open-source framework for metadata-defined business applications: objects, permissions, flows, views, actions, APIs, SDKs, and AI tools. ObjectQL becomes an implementation detail here — the internal data/query engine and driver contract — while the public developer experience centers on ObjectStack docs and the framework repository.

features.title: What Lives in ObjectStack
features.subtitle: AI-ready metadata first; data/query plumbing behind the scenes.

zeroOverheadSecurity.title: Business Metadata Core
zeroOverheadSecurity.description: ObjectStack defines objects, fields, permissions, flows, actions, views, agents, and tool surfaces in one structured model. The runtime decides who can use them and how each action is exposed.

databaseAgnostic.title: Driver-Based Storage
databaseAgnostic.description: Run the same object metadata through SQL, MongoDB, memory, spreadsheets, or custom drivers. The internal data/query engine serves the framework instead of becoming the product story.

virtualColumns.title: Readable by Humans and AI
virtualColumns.description: Objects, actions, and query surfaces are structured enough for the framework, REST layer, SDKs, ObjectUI, and MCP tools to derive useful behavior without duplicate definitions.

typeSafe.title: Typed from Zod
typeSafe.description: Object definitions start as Zod-backed metadata so TypeScript, JSON artifacts, API contracts, UI field behavior, and AI tool signatures stay aligned.

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.subtitle: Use ObjectStack as the open-source foundation; let ObjectOS run it and ObjectUI render it.

localFirst.title: AI-Ready App Models
localFirst.description: Prototype objects, flows, actions, and agent tools locally; move the same metadata into ObjectOS when you need auth, audit, APIs, and governed execution.

enterprise.title: Driver Adapters
enterprise.description: Wrap existing databases, spreadsheets, and proprietary systems behind ObjectStack metadata so APIs, UI, and AI tools can reason about them safely.

saas.title: Product Metadata Spine
saas.description: Provide consistent filters, pagination, actions, permissions, and tool contracts for generated REST APIs, client SDKs, UI renderers, and AI agents.

example.title: From ObjectStack metadata to runtime surfaces
example.subtitle: ObjectStack models the business; ObjectOS and ObjectUI turn it into an AI-operable application.

comparison.title: ObjectStack vs. ORM-Only Backends
comparison.subtitle: ObjectStack is the metadata spine for apps, APIs, UI, and AI tools; the data/query engine stays internal.

runtimeOverhead.objectql: Open-source framework for business metadata, APIs, UI, and AI tool surfaces.
runtimeOverhead.orm: Usually only a data abstraction, forcing custom glue around it.

security.objectql: ObjectOS applies auth, RBAC/RLS/FLS, audit, and agent identity around every action.
security.orm: Governance must be rebuilt per API route and per AI integration.

portability.objectql: Metadata can move across drivers and runtimes as a compiled artifact.
portability.orm: Models often bind to a dialect, migration stack, or application framework.

virtualColumns.objectql: Actions and object metadata feed MCP tools through the runtime.
virtualColumns.orm: Agent tools are usually hand-written and drift from the backend.

drivers.title: Drivers Are the Data Boundary
drivers.subtitle: ObjectStack owns the common object/action contract; drivers own the actual storage behavior.

postgresql.name: PostgreSQL
postgresql.description: Production relational storage through the SQL driver.

mysql.name: MySQL
mysql.description: Relational storage for common web and enterprise deployments.

sqlite.name: SQLite
sqlite.description: Local development, embedded tools, and standalone deployments.

sqlserver.name: SQL Server
sqlserver.description: Enterprise relational systems via custom or commercial drivers.

oracle.name: Oracle Database
oracle.description: Legacy enterprise systems can be mounted without immediate migration.

mongodb.name: MongoDB
mongodb.description: Document storage through native driver semantics where appropriate.

excel.name: Excel / CSV
excel.description: Spreadsheets can be wrapped as governed objects for APIs, UI, and agents.

pricing.title: Simple, honest pricing.
pricing.subtitle: The full ObjectStack runtime is open source — production-ready and self-hostable. ObjectCloud is the managed home for your projects. Enterprise is for regulated and large-scale deployments.

community.title: Open Source
community.description: The complete ObjectStack runtime. Production-ready for a single team — self-host anywhere.
features: ObjectQL & ObjectOS (Apache-2.0), ObjectUI (MIT)
All Drivers: Postgres, MySQL, SQLite, Turso, MongoDB, Redis, in-memory
All adapters: Express, Fastify, Hono, NestJS, Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit
Auth, OAuth / OIDC, RBAC, RLS, FLS, audit log
Flows, automation, and MCP tool generation
CLI, VS Code extension, Studio (self-hosted)
Community support (GitHub, Discord)

pro.title: ObjectCloud
pro.description: Managed home for ObjectStack projects. Push artifacts, run multi-tenant, scale on demand. GA July 2026.
features: Everything in Open Source
Managed multi-tenant runtime (org / project / version)
Hosted Studio + artifact registry with rollback
Auto-scaling edge runtime and global CDN
Usage analytics and observability dashboards
Email support

enterprise.title: Enterprise
enterprise.description: For regulated and large-scale deployments. Dedicated environments, procurement support, and tailored SLAs.
features: Everything in ObjectCloud — or fully self-hosted
Dedicated single-tenant deployment options
Procurement, security review, and compliance documentation
Custom SLAs and white-glove onboarding
Named technical contacts and architect access

hero.label: Enterprise
hero.title: An AI-Native Business Backend, Self-Hosted
hero.subtitle: Run ObjectStack in your perimeter. Same Zod metadata, same MCP tools, full data sovereignty.
hero.description: ObjectStack Enterprise delivers the Data, Control, and View layers as a self-hosted runtime with commercial support, hardened governance, and certified compliance. Define your business once as structured Zod metadata; let humans, services, and AI agents operate it safely under one auditable kernel.

challenges.title: The Strategic Challenges We Solve
challenges.subtitle: Built for CTOs, CIOs, and System Architects deploying AI agents alongside humans.

technicalDebt.title: Agent-Safe Operations
technicalDebt.description: Don't give AI agents raw SQL or a screen-scraped UI. ObjectStack exposes a structured, permissioned, audited surface — REST APIs, typed SDKs, and MCP tools — derived from the same metadata that powers your apps. Copilots inherit user permissions instead of bypassing them.

vendorLockIn.title: No Storage Lock-in
vendorLockIn.description: Drivers connect Postgres, MongoDB, Redis, Excel, and proprietary systems behind one Zod-defined object model. Wrap existing investments instead of migrating them. Switch storage by swapping a Driver, not by rewriting the business.

dataGovernance.title: Unified Governance
dataGovernance.description: One control plane for identity, permissions, flows, and audit across heterogeneous systems. Every action — human or agent — is attributable to a principal and traceable end to end.

assetLongevity.title: Metadata as IP
assetLongevity.description: Your business definition lives as Zod artifacts, not framework code. Runtimes can be replaced; your objects, flows, policies, and view manifests outlast them.

features.title: Enterprise-Grade Capabilities
features.subtitle: Self-hosted infrastructure with commercial support and hardened governance.

selfHosted.title: Self-Hosted Deployment
selfHosted.description: Run the same ObjectStack kernel inside your perimeter — on-premise or in your private cloud. No data leaves your boundary. Full sovereignty with commercial SLAs.

multiTenant.title: Multi-Tenant Isolation
multiTenant.description: Tenant routing, dedicated schemas, row-level security, and encryption at rest and in transit — all derived from metadata, not bolt-on plumbing.

auditCompliance.title: Audit & Compliance
auditCompliance.description: Every REST call, SDK invocation, and MCP tool action is logged with principal, intent, and resulting metadata change. Hardened audit pipeline that maps to your SOC 2 / HIPAA evidence requirements.

customDrivers.title: Custom Driver Development
customDrivers.description: Wrap proprietary legacy systems with a Driver instead of migrating them. Our team builds and supports drivers for your specific infrastructure.

sla.title: 24/7 Dedicated Support
sla.description: Named engineers, direct escalation, custom SLAs. Architecture reviews and AI-agent rollout planning included.

professionalServices.title: Professional Services
professionalServices.description: On-site architecture workshops, migration strategy, and team enablement for adopting an AI-native backend safely.

architecture.title: Three Layers, One Auditable Runtime
architecture.subtitle: Data, Control, and View — driven by Zod metadata, operated by humans and agents alike.

dataLayer.title: Data Layer (ObjectQL)
dataLayer.description: Business objects, fields, relations, validation, and permissions as Zod metadata. Storage-agnostic via Drivers.

controlLayer.title: Control Layer (ObjectOS)
controlLayer.description: Microkernel runtime that loads Drivers, services, and compiled artifacts; enforces permissions; emits REST APIs, typed SDKs, and MCP tools.

viewLayer.title: View Layer (ObjectUI)
viewLayer.description: Apps, dashboards, and actions rendered from metadata — permission-aware and aligned with the agent surface.

comparison.title: Why Enterprises Choose ObjectStack
comparison.subtitle: Compare our approach to traditional platforms

migration.traditional: Forced migration into a vendor's data model
migration.objectstack: Wrap existing systems behind Zod-defined objects via Drivers

lockin.traditional: Proprietary APIs and lock-in by design
lockin.objectstack: Open metadata; export an artifact bundle at any time

frameworks.traditional: Business logic glued into framework code
frameworks.objectstack: Business logic is metadata — runtimes are replaceable

governance.traditional: Per-source security models that drift apart
governance.objectstack: One permission model enforced across UI, API, SDK, and MCP

deployment.objectstack: Self-hosted runtime with full data sovereignty

useCases.title: Enterprise Use Cases
useCases.subtitle: Where an AI-native, permission-aware backend pays off.

shadowIT.title: Shadow IT, Governed
shadowIT.description: Mount business-critical spreadsheets and SaaS APIs as ObjectStack objects via Drivers. Apply enterprise permissions and audit without breaking the workflow.

legacy.title: Legacy System Modernization
legacy.description: Wrap mainframes, ERPs, and proprietary stores behind Zod objects. Expose modern APIs, SDKs, and MCP tools without touching the legacy code.

multiRegion.title: Multi-Region Data Residency
multiRegion.description: Same metadata, region-pinned storage. Deploy identical kernels in EU, US, and APAC; route by policy declared in the manifest.

acquisition.title: M&A Integration
acquisition.description: Connect acquired companies' systems via Drivers instead of forced migration. Unified governance and a single MCP surface across the combined entity.

cta.title: Ready to Operate Your Business with Agents Safely?
cta.subtitle: Schedule a technical deep-dive with our architecture team.

hero.title: Explore the ObjectStack Ecosystem
hero.subtitle: Discover apps, plugins, and universal drivers to supercharge your platform.

projectManager.name: Project Manager

hero.title: Solutions for Every Industry & Scenario
hero.subtitle: An AI-native backend that adapts to your reality — regulated industries, legacy estates, greenfield SaaS, and multi-region operations.

byIndustry.title: Solutions by Industry
byIndustry.subtitle: Built for regulated and mission-critical sectors where humans and agents operate side by side.

financial.title: Financial Services
financial.description: Meet SOX and PCI-DSS with metadata-driven audit, field-level controls, and self-hosted runtimes. Let copilots operate the same backend humans use — under the same permissions.
features: End-to-end audit of every UI, API, SDK, and MCP action
Field-level encryption and PII controls in Zod schemas
On-premise kernel with air-gapped operation
Agent-driven KYC and fraud-review flows under guardrails

government.title: Government & Public Sector
government.description: Modernize without big-bang migration. Wrap Oracle / SAP / mainframe behind Zod-defined objects via Drivers. Operate the same backend with humans and constrained AI agents under FedRAMP-ready controls.
features: Legacy integration via Drivers, not migration projects
Air-gapped deployment for classified workloads
Citizen identity federation (SAML/OIDC)
Multi-language UI projections from one metadata source

healthcare.title: Healthcare & Life Sciences
healthcare.description: HIPAA-ready by construction. Permissions live in metadata; audit is intrinsic; patient data stays on your infrastructure. AI copilots inherit clinician permissions — never bypass them.
features: Audit and consent declared in Zod metadata
Patient data never leaves your perimeter
Integration with HL7/FHIR via Drivers
Granular consent flows surfaced to apps and agents alike

education.title: Education & Research
education.description: Unify SIS, LMS, HR, and research systems behind one Zod object model. Local-first runtime for offline campuses; multi-tenant promotion for districts.
features: Offline-first runtime for remote campuses
Drivers wrap legacy SIS in place
Student data privacy (FERPA) declared in metadata
Multi-institution federation via tenant routing

byScenario.title: Solutions by Scenario
byScenario.subtitle: Architectural patterns for common enterprise challenges in an AI-agent era.

legacyModernization.title: Legacy System Modernization
legacyModernization.description: Don't throw away decades of business logic. Write a Driver for your Oracle, SAP, or mainframe system and expose it through Zod-defined objects, REST, SDKs, and MCP tools — without touching the legacy code.
steps: Write a Driver for the legacy storage / system
Define Zod objects describing the business shape
Generate REST, SDK, and MCP tools from metadata
Migrate flows into ObjectStack as confidence grows
legacyModernization.outcome: Zero downtime. The business runs while the modernization happens.

greenfieldSaaS.title: Greenfield SaaS Development
greenfieldSaaS.description: Start with multi-tenancy, permissions, audit, and an MCP surface built in. Ship vertical SaaS where AI copilots are first-class operators under the same governance as your users.
steps: Model the domain as Zod-defined objects
Declare tenant isolation and permissions in metadata
Project views and MCP tools from the same source
Deploy on ObjectCloud or self-host the kernel
greenfieldSaaS.outcome: Enterprise-ready, agent-ready SaaS without the parallel automation stack.

shadowIT.title: Shadow IT Governance
shadowIT.description: Marketing built a critical spreadsheet. Mount it via a Driver as an ObjectStack object — apply permissions and audit without disrupting the workflow. AI tooling can operate it safely through the same MCP surface.
steps: Wrap with Zod objects and policies
Add audit and MCP tools automatically
shadowIT.outcome: Business keeps running. IT and AI tooling both get a safe surface.

multiRegion.title: Multi-Region Data Residency
multiRegion.description: One Zod metadata definition; region-pinned storage and runtimes. Route by policy declared in the manifest, with cross-region access governed by the same kernel.
steps: Declare routing policies in the manifest
Federate read-only queries through the kernel
multiRegion.outcome: Compliance is metadata. No bespoke residency code.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: ObjectStack AI ("we", "our", or "us") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website objectstack.ai and use our services.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.content: We may collect information about you in a variety of ways, including:
items: Personal Data: Name, email address, and other contact information you voluntarily provide when signing up for our services, subscribing to newsletters, or contacting us.

Usage Data: Information about how you access and use our website and services, including your IP address, browser type, operating system, referral URLs, pages viewed, and the dates/times of your visits.

Cookies and Tracking: We use cookies and similar tracking technologies to track activity on our website and hold certain information. You can instruct your browser to refuse all cookies or indicate when a cookie is being sent.

sections.title: How We Use Your Information
sections.content: We use the information we collect for the following purposes:
items: To provide, operate, and maintain our website and services.
To improve, personalize, and expand our website and services.
To understand and analyze how you use our website and services.
To communicate with you, including for customer service, updates, and marketing purposes.
To process transactions and manage your account.
To detect, prevent, and address technical issues and security vulnerabilities.

sections.title: Data Sharing and Disclosure
sections.content: We do not sell your personal information. We may share information in the following situations:
items: Service Providers: We may share your information with third-party service providers who perform services on our behalf, such as hosting, analytics, and email delivery.
Legal Requirements: We may disclose your information if required to do so by law or in response to valid requests by public authorities.
Business Transfers: In the event of a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, your information may be transferred as part of the transaction.

sections.title: Data Security
sections.content: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal information against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. However, no method of transmission over the Internet or electronic storage is 100% secure.

sections.title: Data Retention
sections.content: We retain your personal information only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this Privacy Policy, unless a longer retention period is required or permitted by law.

sections.title: Your Rights
sections.content: Depending on your location, you may have the following rights regarding your personal data:
items: The right to access the personal data we hold about you.
The right to request correction of inaccurate personal data.
The right to request deletion of your personal data.
The right to object to or restrict the processing of your personal data.
The right to data portability.

sections.title: Third-Party Links
sections.content: Our website may contain links to third-party websites and services. We are not responsible for the privacy practices or the content of these third-party sites. We encourage you to review the privacy policies of any third-party sites you visit.

sections.title: Changes to This Policy
sections.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any changes by posting the new Privacy Policy on this page and updating the "Last Updated" date. Your continued use of our services after changes are posted constitutes your acceptance of the revised policy.

sections.title: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at privacy@objectstack.ai.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Welcome to ObjectStack AI. By accessing or using our website objectstack.ai and our services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). Please read them carefully.

sections.title: Acceptance of Terms
sections.content: By accessing or using our website and services, you agree to comply with and be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree to these Terms, you should not use our services.

sections.title: Description of Services
sections.content: ObjectStack AI provides an open-source, metadata-driven backend for AI-native business applications, including:
items: ObjectStack Framework: Zod metadata for business objects, fields, relations, validation, permissions, flows, views, actions, agents, and tools.
ObjectQL: the data/query engine and driver contract within the ObjectStack Framework.
ObjectOS: self-hosted runtime, permissions, automation, plugins, tenants, artifact loading, APIs, audit, and AI tool execution.
ObjectUI: schema-driven UI engine for apps, views, dashboards, forms, actions, and presentation metadata.
Object Cloud: managed runtime for ObjectStack applications.
Object Marketplace: hub for Drivers, plugins, and applications.

sections.title: User Accounts
sections.content: To access certain features, you may need to create an account. You are responsible for:
items: Maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials.
All activities that occur under your account.
Notifying us immediately of any unauthorized use of your account.

sections.title: Acceptable Use
sections.content: You agree not to use our services to:
items: Violate any applicable laws, regulations, or third-party rights.
Distribute malware, viruses, or other harmful code.
Attempt to gain unauthorized access to our systems or other users' accounts.
Interfere with or disrupt the integrity or performance of our services.
Use our services for any illegal, fraudulent, or unauthorized purpose.

sections.title: Intellectual Property
sections.content: ObjectStack's open-source components are licensed under Apache License 2.0 (ObjectQL and ObjectOS) and the MIT License (ObjectUI). Our proprietary components, branding, and documentation are protected by intellectual property laws. You may not use our trademarks, logos, or brand names without our prior written consent.

sections.title: Open Source License
sections.content: The open-source portions of ObjectStack are provided under Apache License 2.0 (ObjectQL and ObjectOS) and the MIT License (ObjectUI). You are free to use, modify, and distribute them in accordance with the terms of the applicable license. The full license texts are available in our GitHub repositories.

sections.title: Limitation of Liability
sections.content: To the maximum extent permitted by law, ObjectStack AI and its affiliates, officers, employees, agents, and licensors shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or any loss of profits or revenues, whether incurred directly or indirectly, or any loss of data, use, goodwill, or other intangible losses resulting from your use of our services.

sections.title: Disclaimer of Warranties
sections.content: Our services are provided on an "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE" basis, without warranties of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.

sections.title: Termination
sections.content: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your access to our services at any time, with or without cause and with or without notice. Upon termination, your right to use our services will immediately cease.

sections.title: Governing Law
sections.content: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the jurisdiction in which ObjectStack AI is incorporated, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

sections.title: Changes to Terms
sections.content: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. We will notify you of significant changes by posting the updated Terms on this page and updating the "Last Updated" date. Your continued use of our services after the changes take effect constitutes your acceptance of the revised Terms.

sections.title: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact us at legal@objectstack.ai.
